featureList.name: Username and Password Authentication
featureList.description: The Username and Password authentication method is used by users to authenticate with their known username and password.

featureList.name: Social Sign-in Authentication
featureList.description: The Social Sign-in authentication method is used by users to authenticate with their social media account.

featureList.name: Passkey Authentication
featureList.description: The Passkey authentication method is used by users to authenticate with their passkey (FIDO2/WebAuthn).

featureList.name: Email Passwordless Authentication
featureList.description: The Email Passwordless authentication method is used by users to authenticate with a one-time code sent to their email address.

featureList.name: Phone Passwordless Authentication
featureList.description: The Phone Passwordless authentication method is used by users to authenticate with a one-time code sent to their phone number via SMS.

featureList.name: Magic Link Authentication
featureList.description: The Magic Link authentication method is used by users to authenticate by clicking a one-time link sent to their email address.

featureList.name: Anonymous / Guest Authentication
featureList.description: Allow users to access the application without creating an account, with the option to link to a full account later.

featureList.name: Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
featureList.description: The Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP) MFA method is used by users to authenticate with their TOTP code generated by an authenticator app.

featureList.name: HMAC-based One-Time Password (HOTP) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
featureList.description: The HMAC-based One-Time Password (HOTP) MFA method is used by users to authenticate with their counter-based OTP code.

featureList.name: Universal 2nd Factor (U2F) Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
featureList.description: The Universal 2nd Factor (U2F) MFA method is used by users to authenticate with their hardware security key.

featureList.name: WebAuthn Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
featureList.description: The WebAuthn MFA method is used by users to authenticate with biometrics or hardware security keys via platform and roaming authenticators.

featureList.name: Email Code MFA
featureList.description: The Email Code MFA method is used by users to authenticate with a one-time code sent to their email address as a second factor.

featureList.name: Phone Code MFA
featureList.description: The Phone Code MFA method is used by users to authenticate with a one-time code sent via SMS as a second factor.

featureList.name: Recovery Code MFA
featureList.description: The Recovery Code MFA method is used by users to regain access to their account using one-time backup codes generated at MFA enrollment.

featureList.name: Push Notification MFA
featureList.description: The Push Notification MFA method is used by users to authenticate by approving a push notification sent to their mobile device.

featureList.name: Adaptive / Risk-Based MFA
featureList.description: Adaptive MFA dynamically requires additional authentication factors based on risk signals such as device, location, and IP reputation.

featureList.name: Cisco Duo MFA
featureList.description: Integration with Cisco Duo as a multi-factor authentication provider.

featureList.name: Step-Up Authentication
featureList.description: Step-Up Authentication requires users to re-authenticate or complete additional verification before accessing sensitive resources.

featureList.name: OpenID Connect (OIDC) Protocol
featureList.description: The OpenID Connect Protocol is supported by the identity provider for authentication and identity federation.

featureList.name: SAML 2.0 Protocol
featureList.description: The SAML 2.0 protocol is supported by the identity provider for enterprise SSO as both Identity Provider and Service Provider.

featureList.name: WS-Federation Protocol
featureList.description: The WS-Federation protocol is supported by the identity provider for enterprise SSO federation.

featureList.name: Machine-to-Machine (M2M) Authentication
featureList.description: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials grant for service-to-service and machine-to-machine authentication flows.

featureList.name: OIDC Identity Federation (per customer)
featureList.description: Support for configuring per-customer or per-organization OIDC identity federation connections for enterprise SSO.

featureList.name: SAML 2.0 Identity Federation (per customer)
featureList.description: Support for configuring per-customer or per-organization SAML 2.0 identity federation connections for enterprise SSO.

featureList.name: Active Directory / LDAP Integration
featureList.description: Integration with on-premises Active Directory or LDAP directories for enterprise identity federation.

featureList.name: Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) Integration
featureList.description: Native integration with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) for enterprise identity federation.

featureList.name: Bulk User Import
featureList.description: Import large numbers of users from external sources via file upload or API.

featureList.name: Password Hash Import (Multiple Formats)
featureList.description: Import users with existing password hashes in various formats (bcrypt, argon2, PBKDF2, scrypt, SHA, MD5, etc.) to avoid forcing password resets.

featureList.name: Bulk User Export
featureList.description: Export user data in bulk to external systems or for migration purposes.

featureList.name: Bulk User Update / Delete
featureList.description: Perform bulk update or delete operations on multiple users simultaneously via API or tooling.

featureList.name: Upsert on Import
featureList.description: Update existing users or insert new ones during a bulk import operation.

featureList.name: Legacy Username Import (Non-Allowed Characters)
featureList.description: Support for importing legacy usernames containing special or non-standard characters.

featureList.name: MFA Enrollment Import
featureList.description: Import existing MFA enrollments (TOTP, SMS, email) alongside user accounts during bulk migration.

featureList.name: Inbound SCIM Provisioning
featureList.description: SCIM 2.0 inbound provisioning allows external identity providers to create, update, and deactivate users automatically.

featureList.name: Outbound SCIM Provisioning
featureList.description: SCIM 2.0 outbound provisioning allows the identity provider to push user changes to connected third-party applications.

featureList.name: SCIM Groups Provisioning
featureList.description: Synchronize group memberships via SCIM 2.0 for access control across connected applications.

featureList.name: Just-In-Time (JIT) User Provisioning
featureList.description: Automatically create user accounts on first login via enterprise SSO without pre-provisioning.

featureList.name: Lazy / Trickle Migration from Legacy Database
featureList.description: Migrate users from a legacy identity store on first login without forcing a password reset.

featureList.name: Self-Service Profile Management Portal
featureList.description: A built-in end-user portal for managing profile information, connected accounts, and security settings.

featureList.name: User Account Linking
featureList.description: Link multiple authentication identities (social, enterprise, local) to a single user account.

featureList.name: User Blocking / Banning
featureList.description: Block or ban specific user accounts to prevent authentication.

featureList.name: User Metadata
featureList.description: Store custom user-editable attributes alongside the user profile.

featureList.name: Application Metadata
featureList.description: Store custom admin-only attributes on the user profile not editable by end users.

featureList.name: Metadata Size Limits
featureList.description: Configurable or well-documented limits on user and application metadata storage size.

featureList.name: User Search
featureList.description: Search and filter users by profile attributes, metadata, and other fields via API or dashboard.

featureList.name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
featureList.description: Assign roles and permissions to users to control access to resources and APIs.

featureList.name: Organizations (Multi-Tenancy B2B)
featureList.description: Support for multi-tenant B2B use cases with organization-level isolation, branding, and access control.

featureList.name: Password Strength Policies
featureList.description: Configure and enforce password complexity requirements for user accounts.

featureList.name: Username Restrictions
featureList.description: Define allowed character sets, length limits, and format rules for usernames.

featureList.name: Progressive Profiling / Forms
featureList.description: Collect additional user information incrementally during authentication flows using forms or conditional logic.

featureList.name: Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC)
featureList.description: Control access to resources based on user, resource, and environment attributes using policy evaluation.

featureList.name: Fine-Grained Authorization (FGA / ReBAC)
featureList.description: Relationship-based access control (ReBAC) for fine-grained, object-level permissions similar to Google Zanzibar.

featureList.name: API Authorization (Scopes / Permissions)
featureList.description: Define and enforce OAuth 2.0 scopes and permissions for API access control.

featureList.name: SOC 2 Type II Certification
featureList.description: The identity provider maintains a SOC 2 Type II certification covering security, availability, and related trust service criteria.

featureList.name: ISO 27001 / 27017 / 27018 Certification
featureList.description: The identity provider maintains ISO 27001 (information security), 27017 (cloud security), and/or 27018 (cloud privacy) certifications.

featureList.name: HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA)
featureList.description: The identity provider offers a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement for customers handling protected health information.

featureList.name: PCI DSS Compliance
featureList.description: The identity provider supports PCI DSS compliant deployment options for payment processing environments.

featureList.name: CSA STAR Certification
featureList.description: The identity provider holds a Cloud Security Alliance (CSA) STAR certification for cloud security practices.

featureList.name: FedRAMP Authorization
featureList.description: The identity provider holds FedRAMP authorization (Moderate or High) for US federal government use cases.

featureList.name: GDPR: Data Export (Portability)
featureList.description: User data export capability to satisfy GDPR Article 20 right to data portability.

featureList.name: GDPR: Right to be Forgotten (User Deletion)
featureList.description: User deletion capability to satisfy GDPR Article 17 right to erasure.

featureList.name: Consent Management
featureList.description: Built-in support for capturing and managing user consent for data processing, terms of service, and OAuth scopes.

featureList.name: Region Deployment
featureList.description: Available geographic regions where the identity provider can be deployed to satisfy data residency requirements.

featureList.name: Private Cloud Deployment
featureList.description: Single-tenant private cloud deployment on supported cloud providers for enhanced isolation and compliance.

featureList.name: Audit Log Retention
featureList.description: Configurable retention period for authentication and administrative audit logs.

featureList.name: Audit Log Streaming
featureList.description: Stream audit logs to external SIEM or observability platforms in real time.

featureList.name: Security Center (Threat Monitoring Dashboard)
featureList.description: A built-in dashboard for monitoring authentication threats, anomalies, and security events.

featureList.name: Encryption at Rest
featureList.description: All stored user data and credentials are encrypted at rest using strong encryption algorithms.

featureList.name: Encryption in Transit
featureList.description: All data in transit is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.

featureList.name: Customer Managed Keys (BYOK)
featureList.description: Support for bring-your-own-key (BYOK) encryption where customers manage their own encryption keys.

featureList.name: Bot Detection
featureList.description: Detect and challenge automated bot activity during authentication flows using CAPTCHA or ML-based detection.

featureList.name: Brute Force Protection
featureList.description: Automatically block or throttle login attempts after a configurable number of failures to prevent brute force attacks.

featureList.name: Suspicious IP Throttling
featureList.description: Detect and throttle rapid or suspicious login and signup attempts from a single IP address.

featureList.name: Breached Password Detection
featureList.description: Check user credentials against known public breach databases and block or warn on compromised passwords.

featureList.name: Credential Guard (Dark Web Monitoring)
featureList.description: Proactively monitor the dark web for compromised credentials associated with the tenant's users.

featureList.name: Tenant Access Control List (IP ACL)
featureList.description: Restrict access to the identity provider based on IP address, geographic location, or TLS fingerprints.

featureList.name: Device Fingerprinting
featureList.description: Track and evaluate device characteristics to detect anomalous login patterns and prevent account takeover.

featureList.name: Per-Organization Branding
featureList.description: Customize the login page appearance (logo, colors, domain) independently for each B2B organization or tenant.

featureList.name: Per-Organization MFA Policy
featureList.description: Define and enforce different MFA requirements on a per-organization or per-tenant basis.

featureList.name: Hosted / Universal Login Page
featureList.description: A centrally hosted login page managed by the identity provider, ensuring secure and consistent authentication UX.

featureList.name: Embedded / Native Login Components
featureList.description: Pre-built UI components (React, React Native, iOS, Android) that embed the login experience directly in your app without redirects.

featureList.name: White-Label / Full Brand Removal
featureList.description: Remove all identity provider branding from the login experience for a fully white-labeled product.

featureList.name: Localization / i18n
featureList.description: Support for multiple languages and locale-specific formatting in the login and account management UI.

featureList.name: Prebuilt UI Components (SDK)
featureList.description: Official SDK-distributed UI components for quickly integrating authentication flows into web and mobile apps.

featureList.name: Login / Auth Analytics Dashboard
featureList.description: Built-in analytics showing authentication success rates, active users, error trends, and funnel metrics.

featureList.name: SDK Coverage
featureList.description: Official SDKs available for multiple programming languages and frameworks.

featureList.name: Management API
featureList.description: A comprehensive REST API for managing all identity provider resources programmatically.

featureList.name: Authentication API Rate Limits
featureList.description: Documented and configurable rate limits on authentication API endpoints.

featureList.name: Actions / Extensibility Pipeline
featureList.description: Event-driven extensibility hooks that allow custom code to run at key points in the authentication flow.

featureList.name: TypeScript Support in Extensibility
featureList.description: Support for TypeScript in extensibility scripts, actions, or hooks.

featureList.name: Custom Domain
featureList.description: Use a custom domain for the identity provider's authentication endpoints and login pages.

featureList.name: Deploy CLI (Infrastructure as Code)
featureList.description: A CLI tool for exporting and importing tenant configuration to support infrastructure-as-code and CI/CD workflows.

featureList.name: Terraform Provider
featureList.description: An official Terraform provider for managing identity provider configuration as code.

featureList.name: Custom Database Connections
featureList.description: Connect to a custom or legacy user database via configurable scripts for authentication and user management.

featureList.name: Native Webhook Support
featureList.description: Push real-time event notifications to external systems via webhooks.

featureList.name: Universal Login / Hosted Login Page Customization
featureList.description: Customize the look and feel of the hosted login page with themes, templates, or custom HTML/CSS.

featureList.name: Custom Email Provider (SMTP)
featureList.description: Configure an external SMTP server or email provider for sending authentication-related emails.

featureList.name: Email Templates
featureList.description: Customize the content and design of transactional emails (verification, password reset, MFA, etc.).

featureList.name: Custom OIDC Claims / Token Enrichment
featureList.description: Add custom claims to access tokens and ID tokens via extensibility hooks or configuration.

featureList.name: No-Code Auth Flow Builder / Orchestration
featureList.description: Visual drag-and-drop interface for building and customizing authentication journeys without writing code.

featureList.name: Identity Verification / Document Proofing
featureList.description: Verify user identity using government-issued documents, biometric liveness checks, or third-party identity proofing services.

featureList.name: Decentralized / Verifiable Credentials
featureList.description: Support for W3C Verifiable Credentials and decentralized identity standards (DID, wallet-based auth).

featureList.name: Built-in Billing / Subscription Management
featureList.description: Native billing integration (e.g., Stripe) for monetizing access to your application from within the identity platform.

featureList.name: Agentic AI / MCP Server Authentication
featureList.description: Authentication support for AI agents, including MCP (Model Context Protocol) server auth and OAuth 2.0 for automated agents.
